sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: MGW partners with Ribbon to modernize network infrastructure By Investing.com
feed.summary: MGW Telephone is partnering with Ribbon Communications to modernize its network infrastructure and expand broadband connectivity in rural Virginia. This collaboration involves deploying Ribbon’s Circuit Emulation Services to transition MGW from Time Division Multiplexing to an all-IP network, supporting broadband expansion objectives under the Broadband Equity, Access, and Deployment program. Despite this positive development, Ribbon Communications' stock has been struggling, trading at $2.22 with a market cap of $391 million, even after reporting a better-than-expected Q1 2026 non-GAAP diluted loss per share.

feed.title: Patrick Industries And LCI Industries To Combine In All-Stock Merger
feed.summary: Patrick Industries and LCI Industries have announced an all-stock merger that will create a leading component solutions supplier for the outdoor recreation, housing, and transportation industries. The boards of both companies unanimously approved the deal, with LCI shareholders receiving 1.244 Patrick shares for each LCI share. Patrick President and CEO Andy Nemeth will lead the combined company, which expects to generate over $150 million in annual run-rate synergies.

feed.title: SCE Marks 140 Years of Innovation
feed.summary: Southern California Edison (SCE) is celebrating 140 years of powering communities, beginning with electric lights illuminating Visalia's Independence Day celebration in 1886. The company has a long history of innovation, including the adoption of renewable energy sources, the development of hydroelectric facilities, and the electrification of transportation infrastructure. SCE continues to focus on safely delivering reliable, clean, and affordable electricity while addressing climate risks and supporting California's net-zero greenhouse gas emissions goal by 2045.

feed.title: Target replacing Ulta Beauty shops with new in-house concept
feed.summary: Target is reportedly replacing its existing Ulta Beauty shops with a new in-house beauty concept. This strategic shift suggests Target is aiming to strengthen its own brand presence within the beauty retail space, moving away from partnerships to develop its proprietary offerings.

feed.title: What is Giant Eagle? Details as Kroger moves to purchase grocery chain
feed.summary: Kroger plans to acquire Giant Eagle for $1.65 billion, adding 197 supermarkets and 11 pharmacies across five states. This acquisition follows Kroger's failed merger with Albertsons. Giant Eagle, based in Pittsburgh, was founded in 1936 and is the 12th largest employer in Ohio, with 94 locations in the state.

feed.title: Jefferies Adjusts Price Target on Leidos Holdings to $110 From $140, Keeps Hold Rating
feed.summary: Jefferies has lowered its price target for Leidos Holdings (LDOS) to $110 from $140, while maintaining a "Hold" rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst a series of other analyst price target revisions for Leidos, including changes from Citigroup, UBS, Argus, and BofA Securities, with many downgrading their ratings or reducing price targets. The article also provides a company profile for Leidos Holdings, detailing its business segments in civil security, health, energy and infrastructures, and defense.

feed.title: Pearson stock leans on education model. Investors watch the shares into July.
feed.summary: Pearson plc, a London-listed education group, focuses its business model on textbooks, digital learning tools, and testing services for schools, universities, and professional learners. The company's revenue relies on its education and assessment segments, providing recurring income from institutions. Investors, particularly those in the US, look to Pearson for insight into global education demand, with updates primarily found on the company's own investor relations site.

feed.title: FBK Maintains by Raymond James -- Price Target Raised to $63.00
feed.summary: Raymond James has maintained its "Strong Buy" rating for FB Financial (FBK) and increased its price target from $62.00 to $63.00, indicating confidence in the company's future performance. Despite the upgrade, GuruFocus considers FBK to be slightly overvalued at 1.2%, with a current price of $56.93 against a GF Value of $56.25. The company's GF Score is 61/100, showing strengths in valuation and momentum but noting areas for improvement in financial strength and profitability.

feed.title: M&T Business Credit Card from M&T Bank - flexible rewards for small US firms
feed.summary: The M&T Business Credit Card is designed for small and mid-sized US businesses, offering variable APRs and straightforward pricing without complex reward structures. It emphasizes a relationship-based regional service, integrating with M&T's business banking platform and targeting customers primarily within the bank's Northeast and Mid-Atlantic footprint. The card focuses on practical expense management and is positioned as a foundational product within M&T's offerings, contributing to the bank's noninterest and interest income.

feed.title: VVX Maintained by Citigroup -- Price Target Raised to $75.00
feed.summary: Citigroup has maintained a Neutral rating on V2X Inc (VVX) but raised its price target from $72.00 to $75.00. Despite the increased price target, GuruFocus indicates that VVX is currently overvalued by 30.5% with a GF Value™ of $57.53 against its current price of $75.10. The company has a GF Score™ of 74/100, showing solid performance in profitability and growth, but indicating potential caution due to overvaluation and lower momentum.

feed.title: Henry Schein focuses on dental and medical supplies as a diversified health-care distributor
feed.summary: Henry Schein is a global distributor of health-care products and services, primarily serving dental and medical practices with equipment, consumables, pharmaceuticals, and technology solutions. The company's business model relies on efficient logistics, inventory management, and strong relationships with suppliers and customers. Its dental segment is a key revenue driver, complemented by services to medical offices and specialty markets, aiming to embed itself in customers' daily operations through supply distribution and technology offerings.

feed.title: Danaher Corp. focuses on diversified science tools as investors weigh long term growth
feed.summary: Danaher Corp. is a global science and technology company providing diversified tools and services in life sciences, diagnostics, and environmental solutions. The company's business model emphasizes recurring revenue from consumables and services, contributing to stable earnings. Danaher's strategic portfolio management and focus on continuous improvement allow it to adapt to long-term market trends in healthcare and industrial technology.

feed.title: CSX Corp. focuses on rail efficiency as freight landscape evolves
feed.summary: CSX Corp., a major U.S. freight railroad, is prioritizing operational efficiency and an intermodal strategy to maintain its long-term earnings power amidst evolving freight demands. The company leverages its extensive network across the eastern U.S. to transport diverse commodities and offers intermodal services that integrate rail with trucking and ocean shipping for cost-effective logistics. CSX Corp. continuously invests in network upgrades and safety measures, reflecting its critical role in the U.S. transportation infrastructure.

feed.title: Lam Research Corp Stock (LRCX) Moved Down by 8.94% on Jul 1: Drivers Behind the Movement
feed.summary: Lam Research Corp (LRCX) stock dropped by 8.94% on July 1st, driven by sector-wide profit-taking, macroeconomic pressures, and shifting market sentiment in the semiconductor industry. A class-action lawsuit against major memory manufacturers also introduced regulatory uncertainty, impacting Lam Research's forward order book. Additionally, cooling labor market data and rising bond yields contributed to a rotation out of semiconductor stocks, despite strong long-term projections.

feed.title: Kroger acquiring supermarket chain for $1.7 billion
feed.summary: Kroger Co. is set to acquire Giant Eagle for $1.65 billion, marking its first supermarket acquisition since 2015. This deal will add 197 supermarkets to Kroger's portfolio and establish its presence in Pennsylvania. The acquisition is valued at nearly $1.7 billion.

feed.title: Caterpillar Inc Stock (CAT) Moved Down by 4.63% on Jul 1: Drivers Behind the Movement
feed.summary: Caterpillar Inc. (CAT) stock dropped by 4.63% on July 1st, primarily driven by prominent investor Michael Burry announcing a short position due to what he views as unsustainable valuation and a high price-to-sales ratio. The company is also battling structural challenges like declining profitability in its Resource Industries segment, rising manufacturing costs, tariff headwinds, and pressure on its financial division due to high interest rates impacting customer capital expenditures. These factors, alongside recent index reclassification leading to passive outflows, have prompted a sharp correction in the stock.

feed.title: The FirstEnergy Ohio Smart Meter Program - utility roll-out reshapes how homes track power
feed.summary: FirstEnergy's Ohio Smart Meter Program is deploying advanced metering infrastructure to over 2 million customers across its Ohio utilities, with installations scheduled through 2025. These smart meters provide real-time energy usage data, enabling more accurate billing, faster outage detection, and remote service management, and support future time-of-use tariffs. The program is a significant part of FirstEnergy's broader grid modernization efforts, aiming for a more resilient grid and offering customers tools for better energy management.

feed.title: Kroger enters into agreement to purchase Giant Eagle
feed.summary: The Kroger Company has announced an agreement to acquire Giant Eagle for $1.65 billion. This acquisition was confirmed Wednesday morning. KDKA's Erika Stanish is providing further details on this development.

feed.title: Cigna’s Evernorth Makes $100 Million AI Specialty Pharmacy Investment
feed.summary: Cigna's Evernorth is investing $100 million by 2028 into "Pharmacy Forward," an AI-powered program aimed at revolutionizing specialty drug access and managing rising costs, particularly from anti-obesity medications. The initiative, starting with Accredo Specialty Pharmacy, will use AI, data, and clinical expertise to provide personalized patient support, streamline prescription processing, and ensure efficient, same-day/next-day ground shipping for over 90% of patients. This investment intends to empower care teams to focus more on clinical outcomes for patients with complex conditions by improving service responsiveness and reducing processing timelines.

feed.title: Johnson Controls stock hits all-time high of 149.41 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Johnson Controls International PLC stock has reached an all-time high of $149.41, marking a 42.03% increase over the past year. Despite a high P/E ratio, the company has received positive analyst ratings and strategic developments, including a raised price target from Morgan Stanley and a sustained dividend payment tradition. InvestingPro suggests the stock might be overvalued relative to its Fair Value.

feed.title: Cigna’s Evernorth Makes $100 Million AI Specialty Pharmacy Investment
feed.summary: Cigna's Evernorth health services business is investing $100 million through 2028 into a new artificial intelligence-powered pharmacy program called "Pharmacy Forward." This initiative aims to enhance patient access and experience in specialty care, particularly for high-cost specialty drugs. The program will initially launch with Accredo Specialty Pharmacy, focusing on improving clinical care, reducing prescription processing times, and delivering more personalized support for patients with complex conditions.

feed.title: Cintas Corp. focuses on recurring service revenue as investors watch steady demand
feed.summary: Cintas Corp. centers its business model on recurring uniform and facility services revenue, offering stability to investors amidst economic shifts. The company emphasizes long-term customer relationships and workplace safety compliance, providing essential services like uniform rental, cleaning, and safety supplies. This strategy, combined with multi-year agreements, helps stabilize revenue and supports its position as a comprehensive service provider, leading to resilient demand and a focus on long-term growth for investors.

feed.title: Form 144 Walmart Inc. For: 1 July By Investing.com
feed.summary: This article announces the filing of Form 144 for Walmart Inc. on July 1st, published by Investing.com. It is a brief, declarative piece of financial news, common on financial news platforms, indicating a significant insider transaction or intent to sell restricted stock. The article primarily functions as a headline and a link to more detailed data, with minimal textual content beyond the announcement itself.

feed.title: The Neutrogena Oil-Free Acne Wash - Kenvue Inc. targets US teens with a steady skincare anchor
feed.summary: The Neutrogena Oil-Free Acne Wash is a core Kenvue product, widely available in US retailers at a price point of $7 to $10. It is positioned for teens and young adults with mild to moderate acne, offering a recognizable 2% salicylic acid gel cleanser that functions as a steady revenue generator for Kenvue Inc. within its broader skincare portfolio.

feed.title: Citigroup Lowers SLB (NYSE:SLB) Price Target to $63.00
feed.summary: Citigroup has reduced its price target for SLB (NYSE:SLB) to $63.00 from $68.00, while maintaining a "buy" rating, indicating a potential 37% upside. Despite this, other analysts have recently raised their targets, leading to a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target of $60.39. The company recently reported better-than-expected EPS but slightly missed revenue estimates, and has seen insider sales amidst new contract wins.

feed.title: Brink’s and NCR Atleos Shareholders Approve Proposed Merger
feed.summary: Shareholders of both The Brink’s Company and NCR Atleos Corporation have overwhelmingly approved Brink's proposed acquisition of NCR Atleos. This merger aims to combine Brink's cash and valuables management with NCR Atleos' ATM technology, managed services, and self-service financial solutions, creating an expanded platform for financial institutions and retailers. The companies anticipate the transaction will close by the end of the first quarter of 2027, following necessary regulatory approvals.

feed.title: BofA Highlights Pony AI (PONY) Robotaxi Expansion
feed.summary: BofA Securities has reiterated its Buy rating and $19 target for Pony AI Inc. (PONY), noting the company's expansion into challenging operational zones with dense traffic and pedestrians. Pony AI is leveraging its proprietary PonyWorld and Virtual Driver systems to navigate these complex environments and recently launched an autonomous mobility offering in Singapore, planning to expand its robotaxi fleet to over 3,500 vehicles in more than 20 cities by the end of 2026. The company provides a range of AV services, including software deployment and vehicle engineering, aiming for improved profitability through economies of scale.

feed.title: Memory chip stocks hit the brakes at elevated levels: Western Digital dropped more than 10% intraday, and Micron fell nearly 9%.
feed.summary: The article reports a significant intraday drop in major memory chip stocks. Western Digital experienced a decline of over 10%, while Micron's stock fell nearly 9%, indicating a potential pause or correction after reaching elevated levels.

feed.title: Piper Sandler Raises Price Target on Kimberly-Clark Corporation (KMB) on Productivity Gains
feed.summary: Piper Sandler has increased its price target for Kimberly-Clark Corporation (KMB) to $121 from $115, maintaining an Overweight rating. This adjustment reflects manageable cost headwinds due to strong productivity gains and a recent drop in oil prices. The new price target is based on 16x 2027 expected earnings per share, up from 15x, with Piper Sandler anticipating innovation to improve in the latter half of 2026.

feed.title: The Ritz-Carlton, Washington D.C. from Host Hotels & Resorts - business-focused rooms and meeting sp
feed.summary: This article describes The Ritz-Carlton, Washington D.C., a core luxury hotel owned by Host Hotels & Resorts (NASDAQ: HST). It highlights the hotel's focus on corporate and government business, offering 300 luxury rooms and extensive meeting spaces in the West End. The property is identified as a key asset for Host Hotels & Resorts, contributing to resilient demand in the Washington D.C. market.

feed.title: Paychex Inc. (PAYX) Reports Strong Results as AI Platform Supports Future Growth
feed.summary: Paychex Inc. (PAYX) reported strong fiscal fourth quarter and full-year 2026 results, exceeding revenue and earnings expectations. The company also launched its WISE AI-based analytics platform, which is expected to support future growth and margin expansion. Stifel maintained a Hold rating on PAYX, increasing its price target to $110, citing sustained confidence in fiscal 2027 estimates.

feed.title: The Emerald Rain from Sherwin-Williams Co. - exterior finish built for hard rain
feed.summary: Sherwin-Williams has introduced Emerald Rain Refresh Exterior Acrylic Latex, a premium paint designed to provide a self-cleaning, mildew-resistant finish for various exterior surfaces. This product aims to reduce maintenance for homeowners and professionals by shedding dirt during rainfall. It is positioned as a higher-end offering within Sherwin-Williams' portfolio, reflecting the company's focus on innovative, low-maintenance coatings.

feed.title: S&P Global Inc. completed the Spin-Off of Mobility Global, Inc..
feed.summary: S&P Global Inc. has completed the spin-off of its Mobility Global division, with Mobility Global, Inc. now trading independently on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol "MBGL." The separation was finalized on July 1, 2026, stemming from an announcement made in April 2025. S&P Global shareholders received one share of Mobility Global common stock for every S&P Global common stock held as of June 15, 2026.

feed.title: AI data center boom needs renewables — IBM Consulting
feed.summary: An IBM Consulting executive, Arun Biswas, states that renewable energy and battery storage systems are crucial for the Philippines to meet the increasing electricity demand from data centers due to accelerating AI adoption. He emphasizes that while AI contributes to energy consumption, it can also provide solutions for improving energy efficiency and balancing grid loads. Biswas warns that the country's current energy system is not ready for AI-driven demand and requires substantial upgrades, including integrating operational and information technology systems.

feed.title: FDA updates Hologic BioZorb recall, citing 252 injuries
feed.summary: The FDA reported 252 injuries linked to Hologic’s BioZorb radiographic markers, almost two years after Hologic instructed customers to stop using the devices due to adverse events. While the company no longer manufactures these markers, healthcare providers are advised to monitor patients with implants for issues such as pain, infection, or device migration, though removal is not recommended. This update follows a warning letter the FDA issued to Hologic in December 2025 for failing to meet design requirements for the markers.

feed.title: S&P Global US Manufacturing PMI™: Growth Slips to 3-Month Low Despite Expansion
feed.summary: U.S. manufacturing growth decelerated in June to a three-month low, with the S&P Global PMI falling to 53.9, below the forecasted 55.7, despite marking its eleventh consecutive month of expansion. While output and order books showed improvement, firms reduced employment due to rising costs, and business confidence dipped over concerns about the end of war-related inventory building. The article provides background on the S&P Global US Manufacturing PMI, and includes charts illustrating the manufacturing PMI trend and a comparison with the Services PMI.

feed.title: Masco stock holds the line as investors await a fresh catalyst.
feed.summary: Masco Corp (MAS) stock is supported by its strong exposure to the home improvement and construction sectors through brands like Delta faucets. The company's business model is closely tied to housing turnover, contractor demand, and consumer renovation spending. Masco trades on the NYSE, and its financial performance is influenced by the North American repair, remodel, and new construction cycles.

feed.title: Reality Defender is Named a Market Shaper in the Gartner® Emerging Market Quadrant for Deepfake Detection -- Startup Vendors
feed.summary: Reality Defender, an enterprise deepfake detection company, has been recognized as a Market Shaper in Gartner's latest Emerging Market Quadrant for Deepfake Detection — Startup Vendors report. This acknowledgment highlights the growing importance of deepfake detection as generative AI creates new vulnerabilities for enterprises. The company's multimodal platform detects AI-generated voice, video, and images, serving sectors like financial services and identity verification.

feed.title: MCO (NYSE) Form 144 lists proposed Common stock sales by Fidelity, Richard Steele
feed.summary: Moody's Corp (MCO) has filed a Form 144 indicating proposed sales of its Common stock. The filing details that Fidelity Brokerage Services LLC plans to sell 71,511.93 shares with a numeric entry of 174,700,000, and Richard Steele has three monthly proposed sales on April 1, 2026, May 1, 2026, and June 1, 2026, with amounts of 69,167.66, 73,689.62, and 71,679.86 shares respectively. This form serves as a notice of proposed sales rather than a confirmation of completed transactions.

feed.title: Dow Rallies 317 Points On Gains For Shares Of Salesforce, IBM
feed.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose by 317 points, driven by significant gains in the shares of Salesforce and IBM. This performance indicates a positive movement for the overall index, buoyed by the strong showing of these key technology and enterprise software companies.

feed.title: GM sees Q2 gains in U.S. truck and EV segments despite lower quarterly sales (GM:NYSE)
feed.summary: General Motors reported increased U.S. market share in its truck and electric vehicle segments during the second quarter. This growth occurred despite an overall decline in total sales, which was attributed to softer EV demand and inventory constraints. The automaker confirmed these results on Wednesday.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Phreesia, Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against Phreesia, Inc. on behalf of investors who purchased securities between May 8, 2025, and March 30, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Phreesia made false and misleading statements regarding slowing demand, diminished visibility into revenue streams, and weakened pharmaceutical marketing commitments for its Network Solutions segment. Investors who suffered losses are encouraged to join the class action, with a lead plaintiff deadline of July 13, 2026.

feed.title: Boeing to Release Second Quarter Results on July 28 – Company Announcement
feed.summary: The Boeing Company (NYSE: BA) announced it will release its second-quarter 2026 financial results on Tuesday, July 28. President and CEO Kelly Ortberg and CFO Jay Malave will discuss the results and company outlook during a conference call at 10:30 a.m. ET. The event webcast and materials will be accessible on Boeing's investor relations website.

feed.title: Insight rolls out Microsoft 365 E7 across 14,000+ employees
feed.summary: Insight Enterprises has formalized its role as a launch partner for Microsoft 365 E7, Microsoft’s Frontier Suite, deploying it across its own 14,000+ global workforce. This positions Insight as a single partner for clients to deploy the full Microsoft AI stack, leveraging its 91% Microsoft 365 Copilot adoption and offering client solutions through Insight AI. The company emphasizes its firsthand experience in AI transformation, from infrastructure readiness to secure optimization and managed security services like Managed Exposure Defense.

feed.title: Dyne Therapeutics vs. Vertex Pharmaceuticals: Which Drug Innovator Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
feed.summary: This article compares Dyne Therapeutics and Vertex Pharmaceuticals to determine which is a better buy in 2026. Dyne Therapeutics is a clinical-stage biotech focusing on rare neuromuscular diseases with no current revenue but promising drugs in development. Vertex Pharmaceuticals is a profitable, established leader in cystic fibrosis treatment, actively diversifying its pipeline and showing consistent growth. The author ultimately favors Vertex Pharmaceuticals due to its profitability, expanding market, and strong R&D, despite Dyne's long-term potential.

feed.title: Walmart (NYSE: WMT) affiliate files Form 144 to sell 76,181 PSU shares
feed.summary: A Walmart (NYSE: WMT) affiliate has filed a Form 144 notice to sell 76,181 shares associated with an "Exec PSU Share Grant" dated January 31, 2023. The filing also details recent open-market sales by Daniel J. Bartlett, totaling 4,970 shares across three dates in 2026, with proceeds amounting to over $165,000 for each transaction. This Form 144 indicates proposed resale activity and does not itself effect transfers, serving as a transparency measure for significant stock sales.

feed.title: RTX Corporation (NYSE: RTX) Stock Price Outpaces S&P 500 As Earnings Outlook Strengthens
feed.summary: RTX Corporation (NYSE: RTX) has shown strong performance, gaining 1.28% in its latest trading session and outpacing the S&P 500. Over the past month, RTX shares rose 7.41%, significantly outperforming the broader Aerospace sector and the S&P 500. Analysts are optimistic about RTX's upcoming earnings, projecting an EPS growth of 6.41% and a revenue increase of 6.07% for the current quarter, with a Zacks Rank of #2 (Buy).

feed.title: Stanley Black & Decker WARN Act Investigation
feed.summary: Strauss Borrelli PLLC is investigating Stanley Black & Decker for a potential WARN Act violation following a mass layoff of 441 employees in Verona, Mississippi. The law firm alleges the company may have failed to provide the required 60-day notice, entitling affected employees to severance pay and benefits. The WARN Act mandates certain employers to give 60 days' notice before mass layoffs or plant closings to allow employees time to prepare.

feed.title: Invitation Homes focuses on rental housing scale as a U.S. landlord
feed.summary: Invitation Homes operates one of the largest single-family rental portfolios in the United States, concentrating on stable cash flows and long-term housing demand in high-demand metropolitan areas. The company's business model combines real estate ownership with property management, emphasizing market scale for operational efficiency and standardized services. It targets regions with strong demographic trends, providing a professionally managed rental option for households preferring to rent rather than buy.

feed.title: McKesson Corporation Announces Management Changes, Effective August 1, 2026
feed.summary: McKesson Corporation announced that its Executive Vice President, Chief Strategy and Business Development Officer, Thomas L. Rodgers, will retire on August 1, 2026. Ramesh Srinivasan has been appointed as Executive Vice President, Chief Strategy Officer, effective the same date, to ensure a smooth transition. This change follows Rodgers' notice of intent to retire on June 29, 2026.
